title: Binary corporate referendum-style decision over 7 polling rounds

events.description: PRO_LEADER_3 officially announced Long Island City, Queens, as one of the two locations for its new headquarters, sparking immediate AGAINST_SUPPORTER_1 debate
Public reports revealed that CITY City and State officials offered PRO_LEADER_3 approximately $3 billion in tax incentives to secure the deal
AGAINST_SUPPORTER_1 residents and activists held a town hall meeting in Queens to protest the lack of transparency and the potential impact on housing affordability
PRO_LEADER_3 executives testified before the CITY City Council, facing intense questioning regarding labor practices and the use of public subsidies

CITY State Senate Majority Leader Andrea Stewart-Cousins appointed vocal PRO_LEADER_3 critic Senator Michael Gianaris to the Public Authorities Control Board, giving him potential veto power over the deal
PRO_LEADER_3 officially canceled its plans to build the Long Island City headquarters, citing opposition from AGAINST_SUPPORTER_1 politicians and community members

agents.name: PRO_LEADER_3
agents.description: The company seeking to establish the new headquarters

agents.name: AGAINST_SUPPORTER_1
agents.description: Concerned about gentrification and rising rents

agents.name: PRO_LEADER_4
agents.description: Generally supportive of job growth and PRO_LEADER_4 expansion

notes: Blinded: entity names, geography, and absolute dates redacted. Numeric fields preserved.
